---
title: ""
image: ray.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  id: intro
  links:
    - text: Email me
      icon: envelope-paper
      url: "mailto:rhparcon@uga.edu"
    - text: LinkedIn
      icon: linkedin
      url: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/rayparcon"
    - text: Instagram
      icon: instagram
      url: "https://www.instagram.com/ry.parcs/"
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      url: "https://github.com/rayparcon"
    - text: "ORCID"
      icon: orcid
      url: "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-3379-3698" 
    - text: "Google Scholar"
      icon: google-scholar
      url: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=zDKlF7kAAAAJ&hl=en"
    - text: Resume
      url: "resume.pdf"

---

::: {#intro}
<div class="h1">Hi, I'm Ray! 👋🏽</div>

<div class="lead">

I'm a <strong>Graduate Research Assistant</strong> and a <strong>Ph.D. candidate</strong> in the Department of Plant Pathology at the University of Georgia.

My interest in host-pathogen interactions led me to work as a graduate research assistant in the Smith Lab. Together with my advisors, [Dr. Shavannor Smith](https://plantpath.caes.uga.edu/people/faculty/shavannor-smith.html) and [Dr. James Buck](https://plantpath.caes.uga.edu/people/faculty/james-buck.html), we study the genetic diversity of <em>Cercospora sojina</em>, the causal fungus of frogeye leaf spot disease in soybeans, and its implications for host resistance.

</div>
:::

## About Me

I'm a microbiologist passionate about fungal diseases of plants. I became particularly interested in plant pathology when I realized that every strong economy is built upon a robust agriculture. When I'm not in the lab or coding, I enjoy reading fantasy and coming-of-age novels, I think there is beauty both in escaping reality and in stories of self-discovery and personal growth. When experiments fail or my codes are not working, I find comfort and balance in taking care of my plants whether pruning, repotting, or transplanting them.

## This site is currently under construction, but you can find my research papers, presentations, and blog posts here: [rayparcon.github.io/ray-bio](https://rayparcon.github.io/ray-bio)
